title: She’s running Boston to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Dubois, 23, from Hudson, NH, is running the Boston Marathon to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer.
Dubois qualified for the Boston Marathon in September 2025 and is raising money for MGH's Cancer Care Equity program.
The donation program aids families with costs associated with cancer treatments, inspired by care her godmother received at MGH.

title: 1 dead, 2 hospitalized in fatal Brockton house fire, officials say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A house fire in Brockton resulted in one fatality and two hospitalizations early Saturday morning, officials reported.
Firefighters discovered a woman in her 40s dead in a second-floor bedroom engulfed in flames at 80 Glendale Ave.
Investigators deem the fire not suspicious but are still determining the cause, with multiple agencies involved in the investigation.

title: NH suspect who allegedly shot at family members killed in officer-involved shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Masse, 38, was killed in a shootout with police in Raymond, NH, on Saturday night.
Earlier that day, Masse allegedly shot and wounded a Nottingham Police Department officer.
Residents were asked to shelter in place; no other officers or citizens were injured in the incident.

title: She’s running Boston with her best friend after both diagnosed with cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Meg Gannon, a 47-year-old from Canton, Mass., will run the Boston Marathon after breast cancer recovery.
Gannon's best friend Peggy, also a cancer survivor, will run with her for Mass General Hospital.
Both women aim to inspire others battling cancer by sharing their experiences and marathon journey.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Jack Black hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jack Black hosted the April 4 episode of 'Saturday Night Live' for the fifth time.
Musical guest Jack White performed, marking his fifth appearance on 'SNL' as a musical guest.
Black's episode featured a special Five-Timers Club sketch with Jonah Hill, Tina Fey, and Melissa McCarthy.

title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown acknowledged past struggles with his left hand, citing a 2021 wrist injury and 2023 reinjury.
Brown has improved his left-hand game by addressing health issues; scoring career-high 28.7 points per game this season.
The Celtics star's performance without Jayson Tatum boosts him into MVP considerations, securing a top-4 playoff seed.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo returned to Boston with the San Diego Padres, where he has been broadcasting since 2016.
Orsillo was the Red Sox play-by-play announcer on NESN from 2001 to 2015 before being replaced.
During his visit, Orsillo reunited with former NESN colleagues, sharing photos and receiving warm messages from fans.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Alex Cora used Roman Anthony as DH due to defensive struggles, especially highlighted by a recent play against the Padres.
Anthony contributed offensively with a triple and base hit but made a crucial throwing error in left field.
Anthony, former top prospect, is batting .231 this season; Red Sox have a 2-6 record ahead of the Padres match.

title: Connelly Early gave Red Sox a chance to win despite high pitch count, lack of command
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox lost 3-2 to Padres; Connelly Early limited damage despite walking four batters in four innings.
Starting pitcher Connelly Early allowed two runs on three hits but had high pitch count due to control issues.
Early has 39 strikeouts in his first six career starts, tying Red Sox record; he hasn't allowed a home run yet.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-0 win over CF Montreal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution defeated CF Montreal 3-0 with goals from Langoni, Fofana, and Miller, improving their home record to 2-0.
Head coach Marko Mitrović received a red card in the 34th minute for leaving the technical area during a sideline dispute.
Set piece strategies continue to pay off for New England, with Fofana scoring from a Langoni free kick in the 77th minute.
